Politics.headline: Republicans stare down epic voter enthusiasm gap ahead of 2026 midterms
Politics.detail: The Democrats' 14-percentage point advantage is the largest in decades, reflecting Republicans' slippery grasp on power in Washington.

Politics.headline: Trump moves to remake Dulles Airport, weighing redesign proposals
Politics.detail: The Trump administration is considering proposals for overhauling Dulles International Airport.

Politics.headline: Liberals accept Conservative proposals to limit 'immense' cabinet powers in budget bill
Politics.detail: The Liberals have agreed to Conservative proposals aimed at limiting the extensive powers of the cabinet in the budget bill.

Politics.headline: Treasury board president on 4 days a week: 'I think we'll have enough space'
Politics.detail: The Treasury Board President commented on the possibility of a 4-day work week.

Politics.headline: UK suspension of refugee family reunion scheme to be challenged in high court.
Politics.detail: The UK's suspension of a refugee family reunion scheme is facing a legal challenge in the High Court.

Finance & Economy.headline: HSBC bankers to share $3.9bn bonus pot, the highest in more than a decade
Finance & Economy.detail: HSBC bankers are set to share a $3.9 billion bonus pot, marking the highest payout in over a decade, reflecting strong performance.

Finance & Economy.headline: Aston Martin to cut 20% of workforce in effort to save £40m
Finance & Economy.detail: Struggling carmaker Aston Martin is planning to reduce its workforce by 20% in an effort to save £40m, following pre-tax losses of £363.9m for 2025.

Finance & Economy.headline: Consumer confidence rebounds as Americans grow less pessimistic about jobs
Finance & Economy.detail: Consumer confidence is increasing as Americans feel better about the employment situation.

Finance & Economy.headline: European economy shows signs of strength
Finance & Economy.detail: The eurozone economy experienced healthy growth in the last quarter of 2025, with real GDP up 0.3% from the previous quarter and 1.3% from the previous year, driven by both employment and labor productivity growth.

Finance & Economy.headline: Circle’s fourth-quarter revenue rises on strong stablecoin circulation
Finance & Economy.detail: Circle's revenue increased in the fourth quarter, driven by strong stablecoin circulation.

Technology.headline: Samsung Galaxy S26 launch live — all the latest leaks and rumors ahead of today's big Unpacked event
Technology.detail: TechRadar is providing live coverage of today's Samsung Galaxy Unpacked event, bringing you all the news as it happens.

Technology.headline: Samsung Galaxy Unpacked 2026: S26 Ultra Design Leaks, Pricing Rumors, and How to Watch
Technology.detail: Samsung plans to unveil the S26 lineup on Feb. 25 in San Francisco at 10 a.m. PT / 1 p.m. ET and details of what to expect have been leaked.

Technology.headline: Apple will build the Mac Mini in the US and you can probably thank AI
Technology.detail: Apple is reportedly planning to build the Mac Mini in the US, potentially leveraging AI to streamline the manufacturing process.

Technology.headline: Meta agrees $60bn deal with chipmaker AMD despite AI bubble fears
Technology.detail: Meta has reportedly agreed to a $60 billion deal with chipmaker AMD, signaling a continued investment in AI despite concerns about a potential bubble.

Technology.headline: 2 Ukrainians Face Life in Prison for Allegedly Helping Russia Access Starlink
Technology.detail: Two Ukrainians are facing life in prison for allegedly assisting Russia in accessing Starlink satellite internet by bypassing a whitelist for a mere $30.

Science.headline: 40,000-year-old signs show humans were recording information long before writing
Science.detail: A new study reveals that Ice Age humans carved structured sequences of dots, lines, and crosses into tools and figurines, displaying complexity and information density rivaling early writing systems. These signs, analyzed using computational techniques, suggest that the human ability to encode information developed gradually over tens of thousands of years.

Science.headline: Fossil Clues Show Modern Coral Reef Food Webs Have Dramatically Compressed
Science.detail: Isotope evidence from fossil otoliths indicates that Caribbean reef food chains have shrunk significantly. This compression of food webs highlights dramatic changes in coral reef ecosystems over time.

Science.headline: New evidence suggests carbon emissions from trawling may be lower than previous estimates
Science.detail: A new technical briefing and blog post presents evidence on trawling gear and seabed interaction, suggesting that carbon emissions released from trawling are likely lower than previously estimated. The research clarifies what is known about seabed carbon management and how it can balance environmental protection with food security and clean energy goals.

Science.headline: Modern science catches up with native knowledge with discovery of Clerodendrum kelli
Science.detail: A newly described, critically endangered plant species, Clerodendrum kelli, found only in Nueva Ecija, Philippines, was identified through a collaboration between modern science and the indigenous Bugkalot people, who traditionally use it to treat ailing dogs. This discovery highlights the intersection of indigenous knowledge and formal scientific understanding of biodiversity.

Science.headline: Why the Planet Doesn't Dry Out All at Once – Scientists Crack the Mystery
Science.detail: Scientists have been investigating why climate change could trigger simultaneous droughts across multiple continents. This research aims to understand the factors preventing the planet from drying out uniformly.

Health.headline: For disillusioned health care workers in Kansas City, Canada beckons
Health.detail: Changes to U.S. health policy, like the recent overhaul of childhood vaccination recommendations and laws that limit medical care options, are making providers more likely to immigrate to Canada, while Canada is facing a critical shortage of doctors and nurses.

Health.headline: Doctors, health groups call for action as Canada lags on cervical cancer prevention
Health.detail: Advocates are urging the Canadian federal government to improve cervical cancer screening, prevention, and vaccination efforts, as Canada's rate is more than double the rate considered to be the threshold for elimination.

Health.headline: Health 'catastrophe'? Santa Barbara County supervisors back new street vendor law
Health.detail: Santa Barbara County supervisors are supporting new rules and stricter enforcement for street vendors in unincorporated areas, citing health and safety concerns and complaints from restaurant owners about unfair competition.

Health.headline: How will the new GP contract improve the experience at your local practice?
Health.detail: The government has confirmed how GP practices are expected to deliver patient care from April 1, 2026, with £457m to be made available to help recruit more GPs, deal with urgent patients more quickly and ensure GP practices deliver high-quality clinical care.

Health.headline: U.S. Mothers Aren't Recovering From Postpartum Depression
Health.detail: New research shows that U.S. mothers recover far more slowly from postpartum depression than women in other countries, with symptoms often persisting through a child's sixth birthday.

Sports.headline: T20 World Cup LIVE: Weather In Focus As Sri Lanka Face NZ In Must-Win Match
Sports.detail: The weather conditions will be a key factor as Sri Lanka takes on New Zealand in a crucial T20 World Cup match.

Sports.headline: Daniel Ochefu circles back to PBA with Blackwater
Sports.detail: Daniel Ochefu is returning to the PBA to play for Blackwater.

Sports.headline: UAAP volleyball: UST ends winless start, dominates Ateneo
Sports.detail: The UST volleyball team secured a dominant victory over Ateneo, ending their winless streak.

Sports.headline: Brook's special knock guides England into the semifinals
Sports.detail: Brook's exceptional performance led England to secure a spot in the T20 World Cup semifinals.

Sports.headline: Earps says she 'learned some tough lessons' from book backlash
Sports.detail: England's Williamson refuses to rule out strike action amid player overload concerns.

Entertainment.headline: Robert Carradine, ‘Lizzie McGuire’ Actor, Dead at 71
Entertainment.detail: Robert Carradine, best known for playing Sam McGuire, Lizzie's dad, on 'Lizzie McGuire,' has passed away at the age of 71.

Entertainment.headline: Martin Short's daughter Katherine Short, 42, dies at Los Angeles home
Entertainment.detail: Katherine Short, the eldest daughter of comedian Martin Short, has died at the age of 42 in Los Angeles; the cause of death has not been revealed.

Entertainment.headline: Anderson .Paak mixes culture and comedy in film 'K-Pops!'
Entertainment.detail: Grammy award-winning rapper and singer Anderson .Paak is exploring his Korean roots with his directorial debut film "K-Pops!", which is due for release in US theatres on Friday.

Entertainment.headline: Delhi HC grants protection of personality rights to Kajol
Entertainment.detail: The Delhi High Court has granted protection of personality rights to actress Kajol, barring entities from using her name, image, or voice without permission, including in AI-generated content.

Entertainment.headline: Sony Plans to Reboot Its Spider-Man Universe
Entertainment.detail: Sony Pictures has officially confirmed plans to reboot its Spider-Man Universe with a fresh start and "new people" following several box office disappointments, according to Tom Rothman, CEO of Sony Pictures Motion Picture Group.

Crime & Justice.headline: Suspect, four victims dead after stabbing attack in US state of Washington
Crime & Justice.detail: A 32-year-old suspect fatally stabbed four people before being shot dead by a sheriff's deputy near Tacoma, Washington.

Crime & Justice.headline: Waste trafficking driving toxic pollution and harming public health, says UNODC
Crime & Justice.detail: The UNODC reports that waste trafficking is a major cause of toxic pollution that negatively impacts public health.
Crime & Justice.source: United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime

Crime & Justice.headline: Arrests made in multi-million pound waste fraud case
Crime & Justice.detail: Two individuals in Penzance have been arrested as part of an investigation into waste packaging fraud and money laundering. The Environment Agency and Devon and Cornwall Police apprehended a man and a woman, both aged 47, for their role in the offending.

Crime & Justice.headline: Duterte drew up 'death lists' and boasted about murders: ICC prosecutor
Crime & Justice.detail: An International Criminal Court prosecutor alleged that former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte personally drew up “death lists” and boasted about murders committed during his “war on drugs”. The prosecutor presented testimony alleging children were strangled to death.

Crime & Justice.headline: Former San Francisco homeless services CEO charged with misspending $1.2 million in public funds
Crime & Justice.detail: The former CEO of a San Francisco homeless services organization has been charged with misspending $1.2 million in public funds.

Education.headline: UNESCO strengthens capacities in countries across the region to integrate living heritage as a key component of sustainable urban development
Education.detail: UNESCO is working to help countries incorporate living heritage into urban development for sustainability. This initiative aims to ensure that communities can maintain their cultural practices while fostering urban growth.

Education.headline: School Assembly News Headlines Today – February 25, 2026
Education.detail: Schools are intensifying preparations for board exams with revision classes and counseling to manage student stress, while education authorities promote skill-based learning and digital literacy. Cybersecurity awareness programs are being conducted in schools and colleges to educate students about safe internet practices.

Education.headline: DfE Update further education: 25 February 2026
Education.detail: The Department for Education has released updates on English and math progress measures for 16-18 year olds, effective practices for delivering Level 2 English and math, a new service to manage education estates, and funding for the 2026-2027 academic year. They also included a reminder to utilize Apprenticeship Workforce Development program resources before March 31, 2026.

Education.headline: NPO creates pipeline of empowered students
Education.detail: An NPO is creating a pipeline of empowered students who understand tertiary education opportunities and how to approach the application process, demonstrating the effectiveness of their programs. The NPO also facilitates on-campus programs that focus on peer support to help students struggling on campus.

Education.headline: Highly rated teachers would be evaluated once every three years under new bill
Education.detail: Under a new bill in Colorado, tenured teachers rated effective or highly effective would be evaluated every three years instead of annually, aiming to reduce administrative paperwork. Colorado Succeeds opposes the bill, arguing that regular evaluations are essential for teacher growth and student progress.

Lifestyle.headline: A growing lifestyle trend among seniors : why more “cumulants” are choosing to work after retirement to make ends meet
Lifestyle.detail: More seniors are choosing to continue working after retirement to make ends meet, balancing the benefits of remaining engaged in the workforce with the challenges of avoiding burnout and maintaining well-being. They prioritize self-care, socializing, and hobbies while remaining selective about work hours.

Lifestyle.headline: Spain lands two TripAdvisor beach awards in 2026
Lifestyle.detail: Spain has secured two spots in TripAdvisor's 2026 ranking of the world's best beaches, based on the volume and quality of traveler reviews. This recognition places Spain firmly on the global coastal map, with two island favorites making the cut.

Lifestyle.headline: Sayap Suci Opens In Jakarta with Integrated Lifestyle Concept
Lifestyle.detail: Sayap Suci, a new lifestyle and entertainment destination by the Holywings Group, has opened in Jakarta, integrating wellness and lifestyle facilities with a coffee-focused space. The venue includes a scenic lake, shower facilities, and a 1.2 km jogging track, enhancing the overall atmosphere with evenly distributed audio.

Lifestyle.headline: Why bootcut jeans are the coolest jeans of 2026
Lifestyle.detail: Bootcut jeans are making a comeback in 2026, offering a comfortable and chic denim option suitable for various occasions and generations. The modern bootcut has evolved with high rises and fitted waists, providing full movement and a silhouette that feels both agile and strong.

Lifestyle.headline: Harrogate Town Women to face Leeds United
Lifestyle.detail: Harrogate Town Women are set to face Leeds United, as reported by Your Harrogate. This local sports news highlights a key event for women's sports in the Harrogate area.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: How Trump raced past the Supreme Court
Opinion/Editorial.detail: After the Supreme Court ruled against President Trump's tariff policy, he accused the justices of being unpatriotic and sidestepped their ruling by using a different law to impose tariffs, highlighting the speed advantage of the executive branch over the judiciary. The justices took months to reject illegal tariffs; Trump took hours to sidestep their ruling.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The 'People Power' myth endures. But guns still decide.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Philippines' uprising 40 years ago has been romanticized, but the military's backing of the national uprising, rather than 'People Power', led to the ousting of Marcos. Tiananmen Square serves as a better guide, where the military's refusal to break rank resulted in a violent crackdown on protesters.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Trump tariff ruling no surprise
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Supreme Court ruled against President Trump's emergency powers as the basis for tariffs, stating that Congressional legislation was required. Trump claimed the justices were motivated by foreign interests after the ruling, which also noted how Trump's tariff power produced special interest money to lobbyists close to Trump and sweetheart deals for well-connected companies.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Supreme Court tariff ruling spotlights local government limbo
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The U.S. Supreme Court decision on tariffs, along with President Trump's response, may intensify confusion, especially for local government agencies like the Westmoreland County Transit Authority, which faces uncertainty regarding a $64,000 cost tied to a $7.5 million purchase. Riders in Westmoreland County experience tariffs as costs and consequences, highlighting the need for stability in agencies that serve them.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion: NYU can't be IRL on a campus built to prevent congregation
Opinion/Editorial.detail: NYU's new device-free initiative is undermined by the administration's reshaping of the campus with enclosures and increased surveillance that prevent collectivity. The university's actions, such as targeting pro-Palestine protestors and creating an atmosphere that discourages assembly, contradict the call for in-person connection.

World.headline: India's PM Modi arrives in Israel as genocidal war continues in Gaza
World.detail: Modi receives a red-carpet welcome from Netanyahu as he arrives in Israel.

World.headline: Destitute survivors of south-east Asia's cyberscam farms an 'international crisis'
World.detail: Many individuals are facing dire circumstances after being rescued from cyberscam operations in Southeast Asia.

World.headline: Former UK ambassador to the U.S. Mandelson released on bail after arrest in Epstein probe
World.detail: Former British Ambassador to the U.S. Mandelson was arrested on suspicion of misconduct in public office and later released on bail.

World.headline: Trump Says Iran Is Developing Missiles That Can Reach U.S.
World.detail: During his State of the Union address, Trump indicated he was considering attacking Iran if it refused to give up its nuclear program and effort to build long range missiles.

World.headline: Mobile phone use behind the wheel worsens: 'We've seen motorway drivers watching Netflix'
World.detail: Minister makes big play of a cut in funding for etaxis as mobile phone usage while driving increases.

Zimbabwe.headline: Socialisation as the engine of patriarchy
Zimbabwe.detail: Socialization is the strategic manufacturing of consent, replacing a woman's sovereignty with a subservient identity to sustain the patriarchal estate, as highlighted by HeraldOnline.

Zimbabwe.headline: Kenya expels Zimbabwean lawyer over alleged role in protest mobilisation
Zimbabwe.detail: A Zimbabwean lawyer has been expelled from Kenya for allegedly coordinating efforts to mobilize political dissent, according to New Zimbabwe.

Zimbabwe.headline: Passion Java, wife Lily divorce after 10-year marriage
Zimbabwe.detail: Prophet Passion Java and his wife Lily have divorced after 10 years of marriage, according to New Zimbabwe.

Zimbabwe.headline: ZIMRA wins Supreme Court appeal against Pacific Cigarette Company
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwe Revenue Authority (ZIMRA) has won a Supreme Court appeal against Pacific Cigarette Company, reports New Zimbabwe.

Zimbabwe.headline: War veterans–linked syndicate barred from invading miner's claim
Zimbabwe.detail: A syndicate linked to war veterans has been barred from invading a miner's claim, according to New Zimbabwe.
